data.title: Samsung Speeds Up Next-Gen Memory Push for Nvidia
data.description:
Samsung Electronics Co. is moving quickly to stay in front of the AI memory race, with reports saying the company is accelerating development of its next-generation HBM4E product and plans to send samples to NVIDIA Corp. (NVDA, Financials) as soon as next month.

That matters because high-bandwidth memory has become one of the most important pieces inside advanced AI processors. As demand for AI chips keeps climbing, memory suppliers are under pressure to deliver faster, more capable products on tight timelines.

Samsung has already been trying to establish an early lead. It was first among major rivals to begin mass production of HBM4, and now it appears to be pushing just as hard on the next version. The company reportedly showed an HBM4E unit at Nvidia's developer event in March, although the latest report suggests that model was mainly for display.

The bigger picture is that this is turning into a three-way race. Samsung, SK Hynix and Micron are all competing for a larger share of the AI memory market, where strong demand and tight supply have helped lift pricing.

For investors, the story is really about momentum. Samsung is trying to prove it can stay ahead as AI spending keeps reshaping the chip industry.

data.title: Oracle, Amazon to Collaborate on Multicloud Networking
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure and Amazon Web Services are partnering to expand their multicloud networking capabilities.

The collaboration between the two cloud providers will give multicloud customers "a fast, private, managed connection to run applications and move data seamlessly between OCI and AWS," Oracle said.

"This will help our mutual customers modernize their applications, unify their data and unlock new generative AI opportunities," said Nathan Thomas, senior vice president of product management at Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.

The collaboration will allow customers to use the strengths of multiple cloud providers while avoiding the complexities of managing multiple network providers and installing physical network infrastructure, Oracle said.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com

data.title: Roche Plans New Trial for Muscular Dystrophy Therapy to Seek EU Approval
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud

Roche Holding said it would start a late-stage study for a gene therapy for Duchenne muscular dystrophy to seek approval in the European Union after drug regulators in the bloc turned it down last year.

The Swiss drugmaker said Thursday that the new Phase 3 study for the drug, known as Elevidys, aims to generate additional data required for a regulatory resubmission with the European Medicines Agency to make it available to more patients.

The EMA last year recommended the refusal of a marketing authorization for Elevidys, saying the study Roche presented to support its application failed to show the therapy had an effect on movement abilities after 12 months. Roche said at the time it would work with the EMA to explore a path forward.

The company said the new study would test the efficacy and safety of Elevidys compared to placebo over 72 weeks in about 100 boys with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, a rare muscle-wasting genetic disease. The drug seeks to treat the underlying cause of the disease by introducing genetic material with a one-time infusion.

Roche said Elevidys is currently approved in nine countries including the U.S., where Sarepta Therapeutics holds commercialization and manufacturing rights. Last year, Sarepta temporarily paused shipments of Elevidys in the U.S. while drug regulators probed the death of a patient, but resumed them after the investigation concluded that the death was unrelated to the treatment.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: First Capital REIT Agrees to Sell Itself to Choice Properties, KingSett in C$9.4B Deal — Update
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

First Capital REIT agreed to be acquired by and split between Choice Properties REIT and KingSett Capital in a deal valued at 9.4 billion Canadian dollars ($6.84 billion), including debt.

Under the terms of the agreement, the Canadian REIT said Thursday that its unitholders will receive a total consideration of C$24.40 per unit, made up of C$19.24 in cash and 0.3186 of a Choice Properties unit.

Choice Properties REIT is a Canadian real estate investment trust that specializes in necessity-based, urban retail properties. The REIT is controlled by George Weston Limited, the holding company of the Weston family, one of Canada's most prominent grocery and retail dynasties. KingSett is a private real estate firm.

George Weston said it has committed to a C$600 million equity investment in Choice Properties, which will bolster Choice Properties' C$1.7 billion issuance of new equity. The balance will be funded through debt, including the assumption of certain debt of First Capital.

George Weston will still own the majority of Choice Properties, about 58%, and the investment won't affect its current share buyback plans, George Weston said Thursday.

Once the deal closes, Choice Properties will take roughly C$5 billion worth of First Capital's shopping centers and retail properties across Canada, and KingSett will take the remaining C$4.4 billion of assets and will become the new owner of all First Capital units, effectively taking the company private.

The transaction price represents a premium of 17% to First Capital's 20-day volume-weighted average price through April 15, 2026, and a premium of 8% to First Capital's net asset value of C$22.57 per unit.

First Capital's stock price has been higher so far in 2026, rising about 16% to close on Wednesday at C$21.84. The stock is up 35% over the last 52 weeks.

Choice Properties said that it expects the acquisition portfolio to generate full-year net operating income of C$235 million in 2027, with an annual growth rate of about 3.5% in the near-term.

Choice Properties also said it plans to finance the acquisition through a combination of debt and equity.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: Turkey Losing FX Reserves Rapidly by Defending Lira — Market Talk
data.description: The Turkish central bank's efforts to contain the lira's depreciation during the Iran war has led to a significant bleeding of foreign exchange reserves over the past month, Commerzbank's Tatha Ghose says in a note. "This endeavour is not sustainable," he says. If the Iran war drags on, policymakers might have to allow the lira to suffer sharper falls. The lira was already on a clear depreciation trajectory before the war which exacerbates pre-existing balance of payments vulnerabilities and could add to already elevated inflation due to higher energy prices. The dollar reached a record high of 44.8428 lira earlier, according to LSEG, and Commerzbank expects it to hit 55.0 by December. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Prologis Boosts Fiscal Year Outlook as Warehouse Rebound Continues
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

Prologis reported higher first-quarter revenue and raised its fiscal-year outlook as warehouse demand continues to rebound.

The industrial real-estate company reported first-quarter earnings of $1.05 a share, up from 63 cents in the year-earlier quarter.

Core funds from operations were $1.50 a share. Analysts polled by FactSet had expected $1.49 a share.

Total revenue rose to $2.3 billion from $2.14 billion the year before.

"We delivered record lease signings of 64 million square feet in our logistics business this quarter, reflecting the strength of our platform and resilient customer demand," said Chief Executive Daniel Letter.

The company raised its fiscal-year earnings outlook to $3.80 to $4.05 a share, up from its previous forecast of $3.70 to $4 a share. It also boosted its guidance for core funds from operations to $6.07 to $6.23 a share, from its prior outlook of $6 to $6.20 a share. Analysts are looking for full-year core funds from operations of $6.13 a share.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com

data.title: Charles Schwab posts record quarterly profit on client growth, trading haul
data.description:
Brokerage Charles Schwab posted record first-quarter profit on Thursday, lifted by robust client growth and trading activity.

Markets navigated a tough landscape in the first three months of 2026, buffeted by renewed concerns around AI disruption and private credit.

Jitters intensified in March as the U.S.–Israeli war on Iran rattled investors, with concerns over oil supply disruptions reigniting stagflation fears.

Volatility tends to be a boon for trading desks as investors increasingly rejig their portfolios to hedge against risks.

Here are some details:

  • Westlake, Texas-based Schwab's quarterly revenue surged 16% to a record $6.48 billion, underpinned by robust client engagement across its wealth, trading and lending offerings.

  • Daily average trading volume jumped 34% to a record 9.9 million from a year earlier, while trading revenue surged 20% due to record engagement.

  • "Clients continue to turn to us for more of their financial lives, helping wealth and banking solutions reach record levels in 1Q," CEO Rick Wurster said.

  • Investors opened 1.3 million new brokerage accounts on the platform and brought $140 billion of net new assets to the firm during the first quarter.

  • Schwab's net income was $2.48 billion, or $1.37 per share, in the three months ended March 31, compared with $1.91 billion, or 99 cents per share, a year earlier.

  • Schwab provides wealth management, securities brokerage, banking, asset management, custody, and financial advisory services.

data.title: Marsh & McLennan Companies reports $7,597M revenue, $2.36 diluted EPS
data.description:
Marsh & McLennan Companies reported consolidated revenue of $7,597 million for the quarter, with diluted earnings per share of $2.36 reflecting a 15% decline year over year amid higher operating charges. Revenue growth was driven by Risk & Insurance Services and Consulting, while operating results were pressured by a reported $425 million estimated litigation charge and other expense items. The company is pursuing a three-year operational efficiency program intended to reduce annual costs going forward.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $7,597 million, consolidated revenue up 8% year over year (4% underlying).
  • Gross Profit: $1,754 million (reported as Operating Income on statement of income reflects operating profitability).
  • Operating Income: $1,754 million, down 12% year over year due in part to higher expenses including a $425 million estimated litigation charge.
  • Net Income: $1,146 million, net income attributable to the company, down from $1,381 million in the prior year period.
  • Net Income Per Share: Diluted EPS $2.36, down 15% year over year reflecting lower operating income.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth by Business: Consolidated revenue increase was driven by Risk & Insurance (RIS) and Consulting, with Consulting up 11% and RIS up 6%.
  • Channel & Regional Momentum: Marsh Risk grew 8%, with notable international strength—EMEA grew 14% on a GAAP basis (6% underlying)—alongside growth in the U.S. and Canada.
  • Brand & Organizational Update: Rebranded certain operations to Marsh, organizing Marsh Risk and Marsh Management Consulting under that name while Mercer and Guy Carpenter continue operating under their existing brands.
  • Operational Efficiency Program: Launched a three-year "Thrive" program targeting approximately $400 million in annualized savings; the company has incurred roughly $187 million of charges related to this program to date.
  • Litigation & Restructuring Impact: Recorded an estimated $425 million liability related to Greensill litigation and incurred $45 million of restructuring costs, which materially affected operating expenses for the period.

Original SEC Filing: MARSH & MCLENNAN COMPANIES, INC. [ MRSH ] - 10-Q - Apr. 16, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Abbott Laboratories Cuts Outlook, Narrows 1Q Profit
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Abbott Laboratories cut its full-year earnings outlook to account for a recent acquisition and said its nutrition unit continued to notch soft sales during the first quarter.

The company, which sells a range of medical products including heart devices and generic drugs, said Thursday it now expects adjusted earnings of $5.38 to $5.58 a share for the year. That compares with a prior outlook of $5.55 to $5.80 a share, and it is below the $5.60 a share that analysts had forecast, according to FactSet.

The company attributed the decrease in part to its acquisition of cancer-diagnostics company Exact Sciences, which was completed last month and is set to ding adjusted earnings by 20 cents a share.

The roughly $21 billion deal, first disclosed in November, positions Abbott to compete in the burgeoning market for multicancer early-detection tests.

Chief Executive Robert Ford said the Exact Sciences acquisition adds a high-growth business to Abbott's portfolio, "further strengthening our confidence in delivering accelerating growth as we move through the year."

Abbott continues to expect full-year organic sales growth in the range of 6.5% to 7.5%. For the current quarter, adjusted earnings are projected to come in between $1.25 and $1.31 a share, missing Wall Street models for $1.36 a share.

Shares fell 4.7%, to $96.76, in premarket trading.

The outlook came as Abbott posted a profit of $1.08 billion, or 61 cents a share, in the first quarter, compared with $1.33 billion, or 76 cents a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out one-time items, earnings came in at $1.15 a share, just ahead of the $1.14 a share that analysts expected.

Sales increased 7.8% to $11.16 billion, topping Wall Street models for $11 billion.

Medical device sales climbed 13%, to $5.54 billion. Sales across the company's established pharmaceuticals business were also up 13%, to $1.43 billion, while sales from its diagnostics unit rose 6.1%, to $2.18 billion.

The increases were partially offset by nutrition sales, which fell 6% to $2.02 billion on lower sales volumes. The company, which previously said it would cut prices across its nutrition portfolio in a bid to boost demand, expects to improve volume growth over the course of the year.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Acurx Pharmaceuticals Prices $2.5 Million Registered Direct Offering With Concurrent Warrants
data.description:
Acurx Pharmaceuticals entered into a Securities Purchase Agreement for a registered direct offering raising approximately $2.5 million, issuing 816,068 shares at $3.03 per share and pre-funded warrants for 9,017 shares at $3.029. In a concurrent private placement, investors received Series H warrants covering up to 1,650,170 shares at $2.78, immediately exercisable and expiring 24 months after the related resale registration becomes effective. Proceeds will fund working capital and general corporate purposes. The company also engaged H.C. Wainwright as exclusive placement agent, agreeing to a 6.0% fee and customary expenses. The agreement includes a 15-day lock-up on new issuances and a one-year restriction on variable rate transactions, with post-lock-up exceptions for an ATM and a potential equity line.

Agreement 1: Acurx Pharmaceuticals Prices $2.5 Million Registered Direct Offering With Concurrent Warrants

  • Agreement type: Securities Purchase Agreement for registered direct offering and concurrent private placement
  • Counterparty: Investors
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 15 2026 / Apr 15 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Raise working capital and fund general corporate purposes

Agreement 2: Acurx Pharmaceuticals Engages H.C. Wainwright as Placement Agent for $2.5 Million Offering

  • Agreement type: Engagement letter for placement agent services
  • Counterparty: H.C. Wainwright
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 23 2026 / Mar 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through offering closing
  • Reason: Facilitate placement and distribution of the offering

data.title: This Micro Cap Biotech Stock Is Tracking Its Biggest Intraday Gains In 11 Months – Here’s Why A China Deal Has Retail Buzzing
data.description:
Key points:
  • Pevifoscorvir Sodium is an experimental drug that is currently in a mid-stage clinical trial, with further results expected in 2026 and 2027.
  • Aligos will receive an upfront payment of $25 million and could earn up to $420 million in milestone payments.
  • After the deal, the biotech firm expects its current cash, cash equivalents, and investments to support operations into the fourth quarter of 2026.

Shares of Aligos Therapeutics (ALGS) surged nearly 20% in pre-market trading on Thursday, after the biotech firm announced a licensing deal with Xiamen Amoytop Biotech to develop and commercialize its hepatitis B drug candidate, Pevifoscorvir Sodium, in Greater China.

If the pre-market levels hold, ALGS shares would record their biggest intraday gains since May 12, 2025.

Pevifoscorvir Sodium is an experimental drug being developed to treat chronic hepatitis B by targeting different stages of the virus. The drug is currently in a mid-stage clinical trial, with more results expected in 2026 and 2027.

Aligos Could Earn Up To $420 Million

Under the agreement, Aligos will receive $25 million upfront and could earn up to $420 million in milestone payments, along with royalties on future sales. Amoytop will fund development and handle commercialization across the region, including Mainland China, Hong Kong, Taiwan, and Macau. Aligos will retain rights to the drug in other global markets, including the U.S., Europe, South Korea, and Japan.

As a result of this agreement, Aligos expects its current cash, cash equivalents, and investments to support operations into the fourth quarter (Q4) of 2026.

Aligos said that combining Pevifoscorvir Sodium with Amoytop’s Pegbing could yield better treatments.

“Additionally, we believe that combining Pevifoscorvir Sodium with Amoytop’s drug PEGBING®, along with our ongoing ASO collaboration, will enable differentiated combination regimens and more personalized treatment approaches for patients with chronic HBV infection across Greater China,” said Lawrence Blatt, Chairman, President, and CEO of Aligos Therapeutics.

The deal targets a large market, with more than 90 million people affected by chronic hepatitis B in Greater China.

How Did Retail Traders React?

Retail sentiment on Stocktwits remained ‘bullish’ over the past 24 hours, amid ‘extremely high’ message volumes.

One user said the stock is retesting its resistance levels and sees potential upside beyond $12.20.

https://stocktwits.com/YouVSYou/message/650443684

Another user noted that despite a “relatively small” upfront fee, it is sufficient to extend the company’s runway.

https://stocktwits.com/aletz/message/650441794

Year-to-date, the stock has lost around 25%.

data.title: South Africa's Malema faces political crisis after jail sentence in firearm case
data.description:
Key points:
  • Prison sentence would bar Malema from parliament if upheld
  • EFF an outlet for poor, often educated Black South Africans
  • No plan for succession puts Malema party in difficult position
  • Critics say his lavish lifestyle at odds with pro-poor stance

By Olivia Kumwenda-Mtambo

Julius Malema, a polarizing figure in South African politics, faces an uncertain political future after being sentenced to five years prison on Thursday following his conviction for firing an assault rifle in public during a 2018 rally.

Malema, founder and leader of the far-left Economic Freedom Fighters party, built his platform around advocating for the rights of South Africans who feel left out of the country's post-apartheid prosperity.

He has rattled investors with calls to nationalise mines and retake land by force, but his cry for economic justice in one of the world's most unequal nations attracts millions of Black youths, many of them educated but facing a bleak future in a country where one in three people are jobless.

MALEMA SENTENCE RAISES QUESTION MARK OVER EFF'S FUTURE

Malema, 45, was arraigned for firing a gun into the air during his party's fifth birthday in 2018 in Eastern Cape province, in violation of the Firearms Control Act.

He had pleaded not guilty, arguing the gun was a toy, and his lawyers appealed the sentence on Thursday.

His party calls the case a "witch hunt."

If upheld, the sentence would disqualify Malema from serving as a member of parliament - casting doubt on his ability to retain his enduring influence on South Africa's political landscape. His EFF is the fourth-biggest party, with roughly 10% of seats in the lower house of parliament.

"For him now, it might just be the end of the road," independent political analyst Ralph Mathekga said. "The party had no plan regarding succession ... (It is) difficult to imagine the EFF without Malema."

Nicknamed "Juju" by his supporters, Malema was youth leader of the ruling African National Congress.

Malema's refusal to stop singing "Kill the Boer (farmer)" - an apartheid-era resistance song - has been interpreted in far-right chat rooms and by U.S. President Donald Trump as a call to murder white farmers, who own most of the land due to a history of seizures by colonial then white minority rulers.

The courts have ruled against the chant being labelled hate speech, and there is no evidence to support assertions by Trump or the far-right that white farmers are being politically targeted.

FROM YOUTH LEADER TO PARTY FOUNDER

Born the son of a domestic worker in Limpopo, north of Johannesburg, Malema became politically active from an early age and rose through the ranks to become ANC Youth League president in 2008. He founded his breakaway party in 2013 after the ANC suspended him in 2011 for "sowing division".

His supporters are mainly young, Black South Africans - many of them students and graduates who say they are still struggling three decades after the formation of South Africa's "Rainbow Nation".

But his party has made less of an inroad among the uneducated in the slums and poorest villages, owing to his party's failure to address their concerns including water shortages.

Critics have accused Malema - a self-declared Marxist - of hypocrisy, saying his opulent lifestyle, including luxury cars and homes, clashes with his anti-elite messaging.

Malema's frequent response is that he does not need to be poor to champion the poor's cause. In January, in an address to his party faithful, he said: "Being a revolutionary is about the content of your mind and not what clothes you wear or what cars you drive."

Malema has also faced allegations of corruption, which he denies. In 2015, a court threw out money laundering charges against him relating to government contracts.

data.title: Cocoa dips as traders digest demand figures, Barry Callebaut results
data.description:
Cocoa futures dipped on Thursday, having hit a two-month high in the prior session, as traders digested mixed figures on first quarter demand and poor results from Barry Callebaut, the world's top chocolate maker and cocoa processor.

Raw sugar futures recovered after hitting their lowest price in more than five years on Wednesday.

COCOA

* London cocoa fell 0.9% to 2,563 pounds a metric ton at 1206 GMT.

* Barry Callebaut slashed its full-year operating profit forecast earlier, citing supply chain disruptions linked to the Iran war and overcapacity in the cocoa market.

* The Swiss group said its sales volume fell 6.9% in the first half, but it sees a return to volume growth ahead which should bring its full year decline down to between 1%-3%.

* Europe's first quarter cocoa grind, a measure of demand, fell 7.8% from, data showed, while Asia's first quarter grind rose 5.2%. The north America grind is due later this session.

* Dealers said the Europe data was worse than expected/

* New York cocoa fell 0.4% to $3,548 a ton.

SUGAR

* Raw sugar rose 1.3% to 13.67 cents per lb, having hit the lowest price since October 2020 on Wednesday.

* White sugar rose 0.7% to $415.10.

* The front-month white sugar contract expired on Wednesday with a record of nearly half a million tons delivered.

* "(There was) one receiver (of the sugar) and five deliverers, reinforcing the perception of ample product availability (and) weakened physical demand," said broker StoneX.

* "Given this, it is plausible that speculative funds are (expanding their) net short positions, exerting additional pressure on the market."

COFFEE

* Robusta coffee fell 1.2% to $3,353 a ton, though it remains some way off last week's 8-month low.

* "Activity stayed tepid (this week) as farmers are not selling, although demand still exists after prices have come down significantly," said a robusta trader in top producer Vietnam.

* Arabica coffee rose 3.2% to $2.8870 per lb.

* Dealers said while the market continues to expect a hefty surplus next season, there is an increasing risk that the El Nino weather phenomenon will hurt output and cut the eventual supply excess.

data.title: Gold rises as Middle East optimism calms inflation fears
data.description:
Key points:
  • Markets optimistic about Middle East conflict ending soon
  • Investors see 34% chance of at least one 25-bp US rate cut
  • Pullback in oil easing some of inflation concerns - ING

By Ishaan Arora

Gold prices rose on Thursday as growing optimism about a possible end to conflicts in the Middle East calmed inflation worries and improved prospects for lower interest rates.

Spot gold rose 0.6% to $4,819.66 per ounce by 1207 GMT, after rising to a one-month high in the previous session. U.S. gold futures for June delivery gained 0.4% to $4,841.70.

"For the month of March, gold was under pressure because of the need for liquidity in the metal following the war, but that is kind of mostly run its course, that need for liquidity," said Nitesh Shah, commodity strategist at WisdomTree.

Shah added that he expects gold prices to remain very well supported as concerns surrounding central bank independence and dollar debasement risk remain prevalent.

Optimism grew on Thursday that the war in the Middle East may be near its end, with a key Pakistani mediator in Tehran and the administration of U.S. President Donald Trump talking up hopes for a deal that would open the crucial Strait of Hormuz.

Crude oil prices were up more than 1% on Thursday, but remained well below the $100-a-barrel mark.

"Gold remains supported amid renewed optimism around de-escalation. The pullback in oil prices is easing some of the inflation concerns that weighed on prices earlier in the conflict. The move reflects a broader shift in market focus," ING analysts said.

Global equities vaulted past their previous all-time highs in Asian trading as optimism around a Middle East peace deal grew.

Gold prices fell to as low as $4,097.99 an ounce on March 23 as high inflation concerns due to soaring energy prices raised expectations of a more hawkish approach to interest rates by the U.S. Federal Reserve, weighing on the non-yielding metal's demand.

Prices have since recovered as investors now see a more than 34% chance of at least one U.S. interest rate cut by 2026-end, up from 32% a day earlier, as per CME's FedWatch Tool.

Among other metals, spot silver rose 0.7% to $79.59 per ounce, platinum gained 1.5% to $2,141.45 and palladium was up 0.8% at $1,586.05.
